item.title: Re: What a wacky GAW adventure bro
item.content:encoded: Don’t post comments on my articles.

item.title: Re: What a wacky GAW adventure bro
item.content:encoded: What does this have to do with the article?

item.title: Re: What a wacky GAW adventure bro
item.content:encoded: That is interesting. I'm sure they are very different, but to be fair, Liber could be a trans woman. Liber keeps that information private. The Nazi thing though is definitive.

item.title: Re: What a wacky GAW adventure bro
item.content:encoded: Bones’s entire personality is actually based on a trans woman I know. So very different. I can definitely see the surface comparison though. I think their stances on Nazis in their communities are quite different, however.

item.title: What a wacky GAW adventure bro
item.content:encoded:
+1, thoroughly enjoyed it.

In my headcanon, bones is Liberonscien. Like it’s uncanny. They are literally the same person.

Some minor nitpicks:

+ show block
– hide block

Description: SCP-2293 is a phenomenon in which media within a given area (up to 20km2) that contains any form of dialogue or speech may be subject to alterations. The area's baseline Hume rating decreases in direct proportion to the quantity of media affected by SCP-2293. SCP-2293 appears irregularly, and no method for prediction of future manifestations has been devised.

Might just be me, but the first line felt a bit ambiguous. It left me thinking that the given area was originating from some physical anomaly. I had to backtrack once I realized that was not the case. I would suggest rewording the paragraph, moving the beginning of the last line. See below.

Description: SCP-2293 is an irregularly appearing phenomenon in which media within a given area (up to 20km2) that contains any form of dialogue or speech may be subject to alterations. The area's baseline Hume rating decreases in direct proportion to the quantity of media affected by SCP-2293. No method for prediction of future SCP-2293 manifestations has been devised.

To continue…

SCP-2293 is also able to change media as it is broadcast, whether live or not. This includes live broadcasts but also “reruns” of previously taped media.

“whether live or not” is redundant as it is made obvious in the next line. Also, I think it would sound more clinical if you got rid of the “reruns” and replaced it with “reshowing”, and without the quotes. It rubs me the wrong way when using quotes like that to force in a non-clinical word when an alternative exists.
Just to be clear, this is what I mean.

SCP-2293 is also able to change media as it is broadcast. This includes live broadcasts but also reshowings of previously taped media.

Here we go agaiiiiiin

Upon the ending of the SCP-2293 phenomenon, all affected media remain changed.

Shouldn’t “remain” be changed to “remains”? I’m not sure.


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a Gay-mers against Weed skip. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The author chose to use a custom theme for their article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is the SCP Foundation logo colored with a rainbow in this skip? It doesn't happen to me in the other articles… Is this an anomalous property of this skip?

item.title: I’m getting to like Jude Kriyot
item.content:encoded:
It’s actually making GAW a little less obnoxious for me. Or maybe it’s obnoxious still but it’s funny too now?

+1

item.title: Admin Post - Closed
item.content:encoded: No roleplaying on the forums, Scan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You’ve totally misread this article spectacularly, dude. If you think Stephen King literally “made a joke” about his car accidents and I’m just piggy backing it, you’re out of your mind. I don’t even understand what you’re saying. Because Stephen King wrote about it happening to him I can’t write about the prevalence of it in his works? Do you understand the piece? Did you read the entire thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Its low quality, but its low quality on purpose thus its ironic.

"Low quality" is a subjective thing in this case, and you can't say that something you think was subjective is on purpose (objective). That's not how critique works.

that includes the oh-so-funny extrademsnional portal in a mans asshole of SCP-3015.

Such as this, which isn't "low quality on purpose", nor meant to be a meme. (Actual quality of course varies from person to person.) It's not being "passed off as good".

If you take away the GoI for these entries what you are left with are really shitty articles.

This is actually pretty close to an actual critique! Which is true. GoIs suffer in general from being superfluous in what they are featured in, to the point where removing them doesn't ultimately matter much. It's what the author does with each GoI that really makes a difference. You pinpoint this (though in a way that is being a total jackass), yet can't recognize the other problems with your critique?

How long before the content of GAW SCPs become acceptable content even whout the GoI?

It's been a year and two months now since the group was introduced to the site. It's been business as usual. No need to fearmonger, my pal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ No
- Roleplaying

gamers against weed will singlehandedly pussify the scp wiki with 1,000,000 ironic shitposts and force it to accept mary sue self inserts and also delete all of the scary articles and stop me from getting a girlfriend

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And there in lies the gateway. How long before the content of GAW SCPs become acceptable content even whout the GoI?

If it doesn't have the GoI, it's not a GAW SCP anymore, is it?

Also, I can't speak for the audience as a whole, but I'm inclined to trust them not to just welcome in a ton of low-quality work en masse just because GAW stuff exists, considering:

  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-4419650/deletions-47:you-no-take-candle
  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-3883830/deletions-46:he-s-wild-you-know-not-like-a-tame-lion
  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-3170038/deletions-45:west-mewnian-swamp-water
  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-2302957/deletions-44:ar-logh-qoy-lu-pu
  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-2255194/deletions-43:cogito-ergo-sum
  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-2211417/deletions-42:the-answer-to-life-the-universe-and-everything
  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-2155378/deletions-41:metaphors-have-a-way-of-holding
  • etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Effort shitposting is the same thing as ironic shitposting. Its low quality, but its low quality on purpose thus its ironic.
The copypastas on /x/ that this site owes its birth to were not shitposts, they were good OC.

As for 2293
Its a mediocre and unoriginal joke SCP being passed off as a mainline SCP because it was made by a GoI that makes mediocre joke SCPs
Its unoriginal as the characters in 2293 themselves acknowledge, King himself may have referenced the fact that he was hit by a car in some of his later works. Its a "joke" that has already been made by King and its not even a joke.

As for GAW, this kind of thing is its calling card.
SCP-1168 is a youtube meme
They have a line of Little Mister knock offs that includes the oh-so-funny extrademsnional portal in a mans asshole of SCP-3015.

These are bad SCPs that are passed off as good because they are made to be bad.
That is what makes them ironic. Its a bad SCP, but its bad on purpose.
If you take away the GoI for these entries what you are left with are really shitty articles.
And there in lies the gateway. How long before the content of GAW SCPs become acceptable content even whout the GoI?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also this whole site was inspired by a 4chan effort shitpost so I don't think this is a new gateway to anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Name one thing ironic about this. Everything here is entirely one thousand percent sincere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, it's obvious from the writing that there was effort and time put into it to make it consistent and an actual narrative story. I dunno if it's shitposting at that point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ironic shitposting is still shitposting. This type of thing is a gateway for low quality content becoming acceptable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's because it can't really be contained, they can't predict where it's gonna show up, and also it's a huge pain in the ass to clean up once it shows up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: didn't mean to reply to you the-carrot-clarinet, apologies!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i adore this, it's funny and weird and incredibly human But….. why is it keter? is it simply because it Cannot be contained? because i don't see this posing a threat to humanity. may have read it wrong.

item.content:encoded:
So, did you know that world-renowned writer Stephen King was once hit by a car? Just something to consider.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh shit I didn't even notice that, I thought they were just referencing the actual site janitors

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually laughed out loud imagining this, making my cat bolt out of the room.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't like GAW at first, but they've grown on me.

harmpit: waht is it i dont click links

I freaking love Harmpit

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like Anderson Robotics (._.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The author appeared to have corrected it, so no issue here.

Technically, if if is spelling, grammar and punctuation correction (unless the supposed "incorrect" terms are intentional), those edits are alright. It is helpful to state the nature of the edit so that the author knows what is going on.

In this case, it's more of a continuity issue. It is indeed good to double-check with the author for less obvious parts like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You would be right. Should we edit it, author/mods?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"gapcopmp4: this is bullshit and im outta here for tonight."
I think it's 'gaycopmp4'?

I would edit myself, but I dont know the rules for that and stuff. I rather not risk messing up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the SCP in itself, but I personally found the chatlogs tedious and hard to read. Still, +1

item.title: 20 minutes into the future
item.content:encoded:
Made me laugh, and I will second Communism_will_win's points about the setting of this skip. It is very contemporary in a way that very few articles try to be, and that sets it apart.

I think we are all too immersed in the Internet's demimondes (IRC, reddit, 4chan, etc, and the commentariat surrounding them) to take a step back and appreciate how utterly bizarre they are.

+1

item.title: Staffpost- Closed
item.content:encoded: You've been around long enough to know what's acceptable and what isn't, thefran. Stop being a cock.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What are you talking about?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can criticize without resorting to political insults.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fucking smug and inept extremely condescending leftist memers and their insultingly unfunny maymaymaymays

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knew it was something inhuman, but I hadn't read 2721 so my guess was some GAW AIC. And now I want there to be a GAW AIC because that seems like something they would absolutely do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really funny and well done
+1, you made me laugh out loud in art class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There isn't any actual threat to human life. But there IS a threat to having widespread panic and confusion when the property of people are somehow manipulated inside their homes, news networks and television shows and radios everywhere broadcasted the same cryptic message about Stephen King, all while no normal organization will be able to claim "lol it was just a social experiment."

Not to mention the anomaly itself is an annoyingly hard to contain phenomenon. Hell, I had trouble tagging this because I had to realize that there wasn't any known physical element to this thing other than its effects.

So yeah, Gamers Against Weed is doing this for shits and giggles, but the Foundation have every reason to be concerned with a GOI who can target 20km3 areas with memetic information.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the foundation's response to this scp seems to be so out of proportion to the actual threat that it is entirely in character.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. Love all the clues there, too- obvious in hindsight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fact that Bones is actually SCP-2721 makes this at least 36 times better.

I wish I could upvote 36 more times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But in different ways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So they're both dicks. Okay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gamers Against Weed is the first 21st-century GOI (shut up Anderson Robotics, nobody likes you). Most SCPs float off in some sort of timeless void where they work equally well in 2016 as they do in 1996 and 1976, except when they mention computers or the internet, but even then the internet is often just a vector for the SCP in question or a throwaway line in the containment procedures.

You can make most of the other GOIs work in the present day, but if they're part of a larger social context at all they're distinctly 20th-century, if not older. Gamers Against Weed's inclusion says that a story takes place HERE and NOW, and it's about relationships and trends and movements with which the reader can relate to at best and identify at worst.

By comparison, Are We Cool Yet is what a bunch of gamers and students think dada is and/or when they want to be meta. Not that good things can't come of it, but its inclusion in an article sets an entirely different tone than Gamers Against Weed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think AWCY? articles come from a certain kind of "meanness" against the normies. Even the "more playful" AWCY? articles are still arrogant and pretentious. They're much more interested in letting you know how smart they are than anything else. They're amoral.

I look at GAW as being a group with a heart that is ultimately in it for the joke. It's not fun to have completely amoral trolls. I want a cast of anomalous internet shitposters who actually have real human connections with people and want to foster goodness in the world, however way they see it. But, like, still, at the end of the day, they're married to the joke, and they don't take themselves as deathly seriously as AWCY? does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd suggest looking at them this way: if AWCY is the guy at a party that won't shut up about "first world problems", GAW is the guy responsible for AWCY guy waking up in a field with no shoes and a hello kitty tattoo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My qualm being, we've already got AWCY. What does GAW contribute to overall canon that's different?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same! That's why I tried to focus on the group as a bunch of friends who genuinely like each other. Trying to get to the humanity behind the "memes."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see this quickly becoming an oversaturation of "le memes", and to be frank I'm not looking forward to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most GAW people I'd say would be the magic equivalent of script kiddies, but bluntfiend is straight up a reality bender.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My impression is that AWCY = Oft pretentious anomalous radicals with an artistic bent, while GAW = hacktivist IRC trolls that dabble in magic for kicks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've always found that the vast majority of AWCY articles that I dislike involve them killing people. That depiction of the group feels distasteful and hamfisted into making them ~*~obviously evil~*~ and making them "crazy artists".

(This has nothing to do with my opinion of this skip, just thought I'd chime in.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say they're more "political" and less, like, eager to kill civilians than AWCY? but I can understand that other facets of the group haven't exactly had time to ~Shiiiine~ yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are a lot of things to like here.

  • "+1 for anomalous pranks and tricks" is a good way to sum up my feelings about this — a dumb online inside joke getting waaay out of hand is a great premise for an SCP.
  • It memorably renders chatroom banter — everything from the usernames to the jokes to the cadence is larger than life in a good way.
  • The eponymous inside joke is, itself, incredibly funny for no reason I can adequately explain.
  • Lastly, I really like this take on Gamers Against Weed — while my 2842 sorta set the tone for them, 2293 does the heavy lifting of their actual characterization: sorta out-of-control, but very much human; disruptive, but with a good heart and a love of fun; extremely online, but not gratingly so. It's everything I had hoped for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not feeling GAW. It's like AWCY, but with shitposting and memes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for anomalous pranks and tricks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: holy shit this one i didn't actually know and makes this even funnier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Low-level moderators, especially on 4chan, are sometimes called janitors

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "janitors" ≠ "jailors"
